CREATE TABLE test_list (id INTEGER, values ARRAY<INTEGER>);
INSERT INTO test_list VALUES (1, [3, 6, 1, 2]), (2, [5, NULL, 7, 1]), (3, [NULL, NULL]), (4, []);
SELECT id, list_sort(values) AS sorted_values FROM test_list;
SELECT id, list_sort(values) AS sorted_values FROM test_list WHERE id = 1;
SELECT id, list_sort(values) AS sorted_values FROM test_list WHERE values IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, list_sort(values) AS sorted_values FROM test_list WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_list WHERE id > 1);
SELECT id, list_sort(values) AS sorted_values FROM (SELECT id, values FROM test_list WHERE id <= 2) AS subquery;
CREATE TABLE another_test (other_id INTEGER, other_values ARRAY<INTEGER>);
SELECT a.id, list_sort(a.values) AS sorted_values, b.other_id, list_sort(b.other_values) AS sorted_other_values FROM test_list a JOIN another_test b ON a.id = b.other_id;
SELECT id, list_sort(values) AS sorted_values FROM test_list ORDER BY sorted_values DESC;
